subject: First Aid & Safety
title: How Foodborne Illness Works
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: How Foodborne Illness Works.

hook.text: 1 in 6 Americans gets food poisoning every year. Most blame 'the flu'—it's usually the food.

buildup.text: Bacteria like Salmonella and E. coli contaminate food at any point from farm to fork.

discovery.text: Toxins from bacteria damage gut lining cells, causing vomiting, diarrhea, and cramps.

twist.text: Most food poisoning is self-limiting, but it kills 3,000 Americans per year—mostly the vulnerable.

climax.text: Proper cooking temperatures and handwashing prevent the vast majority of cases.

punchline.text: That 'stomach bug' was probably last night's chicken. Cook it right.

quiz.question: How many Americans get food poisoning each year?
